name: password_reset_link

metadata.description: Password reset email that delivers a reset token intended to be used as a link parameter (used by on_user_forgot when method is 'link' or 'email').
notes: `user` is a dict (user.to_dict('basic')) auto-injected by User.send_template_email if missing.
This framework does not define the frontend reset URL. This template includes the token and a placeholder link pattern that projects can customize.
Keep wording neutral and avoid leaking account existence; endpoint already returns a generic message.

text_template:
Hello{% if user.display_name %} {{ user.display_name }}{% endif %},

A password reset was requested for your account.

Use this token to reset your password:
{{ token }}

If your project supports reset links, your reset URL typically looks like:
https://YOUR_APP_HOST/reset-password?token={{ token }}

If you didn't request a password reset, you can ignore this email.
html_template:
Reset your password

Hello{% if user.display_name %} {{ user.display_name }}{% endif %},

A password reset was requested for your account.

Use the token below to reset your password:

Reset token
{{ token }}

If your project supports reset links, your reset URL typically looks like:
https://YOUR_APP_HOST/reset-password?token={{ token }}

If you didn't request a password reset, you can ignore this email.

Sent by Django-MOJO
